metadata.name: AU→UK Chilled Beef Export Corridor — Instance Data
metadata.domain: International Meat Trade / Cold Chain Logistics
metadata.description: Complete graph of the Queensland abattoir → Port of Brisbane → Suez → Felixstowe → UK distribution corridor for chilled grass-fed beef. Includes 9 chain nodes, 7 legs, 12 parties, 8 compliance gates, 14 trade documents, 9 bottlenecks with risk assessments and net impact, 5 hypotheses, 4 scenarios, 6 cascade effects, 4 predictive patterns, 3 incident examples with escalation chains.

examples.name: Queensland Grass-Fed Beef → UK Retail
examples.description: Chilled grass-fed beef from DAFF-accredited abattoir in Toowoomba QLD to UK supermarket distribution via Port of Brisbane and Felixstowe. 90-day shelf life from slaughter, ~35-40 day transit, leaving 50-55 days for UK retail. AU-UK FTA preferential tariff (0% on beef quota). CIF Felixstowe terms.
examples.commodity: Chilled grass-fed beef (primals and subprimals, vacuum-packed)
examples.regulatoryRegime: AU-UK Free Trade Agreement 2023; AU Export Control Act 2020; UK BTOM (Border Target Operating Model) 2024; EU-derived SPS standards

examples.name: Toowoomba Abattoir & Processing
examples.capabilities: slaughter, boning, vacuum-pack, blast-chill, DAFF-accredited, halal-certified, MSA-graded

examples.name: Toowoomba Cold Storage (Pre-Haulage Staging)
examples.capabilities: 0-2C holding, container pre-loading, phytosanitary inspection staging

examples.name: Felixstowe Border Control Post (BCP)
examples.operator: APHA (Animal and Plant Health Agency) / Port Health Authority

examples.name: Daventry National Distribution Centre (Cold Store)
examples.location: DIRFT (Daventry International Rail Freight Terminal), Northamptonshire

examples.name: UK Supermarket Regional DC
examples.location: Various (Tesco Didcot / Sainsbury's Basingstoke / M&S Castle Donington)

examples.route: Brisbane then Coral Sea then Torres Strait then Singapore
Singapore then Malacca Strait then Indian Ocean then Red Sea then Suez Canal then Mediterranean then English Channel then Felixstowe
examples.insuranceCoverage: Marine Cargo All-Risk (ICC-A) + War Risk Surcharge, AUD 250,000

examples.name: Darling Downs Beef Pty Ltd
examples.role: Meat processor and exporter — slaughter, processing, packing, export documentation
examples.responsibilities: Product quality, DAFF compliance, health certificate application, container loading, shelf-life guarantee
examples.riskOwnership: Product quality to FOB. Cold chain to FCA Toowoomba. Regulatory compliance (AU side).
examples.licences: DAFF Registered Establishment AU-1247, Halal Certification HC-QLD-0891, MSA Licence

examples.name: British Meat Imports Ltd
examples.role: UK importer of record — customs clearance, IPAFFS notification, BCP liaison, distribution
examples.responsibilities: UK import compliance, IPAFFS pre-notification, CHED-P submission, duty payment, cold storage, retailer delivery
examples.riskOwnership: All risk from CIF Felixstowe. UK regulatory compliance. Storage and distribution costs.
examples.licences: HMRC EORI GB-928471623000, IPAFFS Registered Importer, DEFRA Approved

examples.name: SCT Logistics (Freight Forwarding Division)
examples.role: Origin freight forwarder — booking, documentation coordination, container logistics
examples.responsibilities: Carrier booking, container pre-cool verification, export customs lodgement, B/L instruction, insurance arrangement
examples.riskOwnership: Operational coordination. No cargo risk (agent only).

examples.name: Hapag-Lloyd AG
examples.role: Ocean carrier — Brisbane to Felixstowe via Singapore transhipment
examples.responsibilities: Vessel operation, reefer container supply, temperature monitoring at sea, on-time arrival, B/L issuance
examples.riskOwnership: Carrier liability under Hague-Visby Rules (SDR 2 per kg or SDR 666.67 per package). Reefer machinery breakdown.

examples.name: Expedient Customs Brokers Pty Ltd
examples.role: Australian customs broker — export declaration, tariff classification, FTA origin cert
examples.responsibilities: Export declaration lodgement (ICS), FTA Certificate of Origin preparation, duty drawback claims
examples.riskOwnership: Declaration accuracy. Penalty liability for misclassification.

examples.name: Clearfreight UK Ltd
examples.role: UK customs broker — import declaration, BTOM compliance, duty/VAT
examples.responsibilities: Import customs declaration (CDS), tariff classification, FTA preference claim, duty payment, BTOM compliance

examples.name: DAFF (Department of Agriculture, Fisheries & Forestry)
examples.role: Australian export regulatory authority — meat inspection, health certification, establishment audit
examples.responsibilities: Ante/post-mortem inspection, health certificate issuance, export permit, establishment registration, residue testing
examples.riskOwnership: Regulatory authority — sets the rules. Non-negotiable.
examples.contactSLA: Government SLA (2-5 business days standard, 24 hours urgent)

examples.name: APHA (Animal & Plant Health Agency)
examples.role: UK border SPS authority — import inspection, health certificate verification, physical checks
examples.responsibilities: CHED-P verification, identity checks, physical inspection (random and risk-based), laboratory sampling, import authorisation
examples.riskOwnership: Regulatory authority — can reject, detain, or destroy non-compliant consignments.

examples.name: Toll Refrigerated Transport
examples.role: Domestic reefer haulage — Toowoomba to Port of Brisbane
examples.responsibilities: Reefer truck operation, temperature maintenance, GPS tracking, timely delivery to port cut-off

examples.name: Reed Boardall Transport Ltd
examples.role: UK inland reefer haulage — Felixstowe to Daventry and onwards
examples.responsibilities: Container collection from BCP, reefer temperature maintenance, delivery to cold store, empty container return
examples.riskOwnership: In-transit temperature UK side. Delay to cold store.

examples.name: Lineage Logistics UK
examples.role: UK cold storage and distribution — stockholding, pick-pack, retailer delivery prep
examples.responsibilities: Temperature-controlled storage, stock rotation (FIFO), date-code management, retail-ready packaging, order fulfilment
examples.riskOwnership: Storage temperature integrity. Stock loss through date expiry.

examples.name: DAFF Health Certificate & Export Permit
examples.commonFailureReasons: Residue test positive (MRL breach), document mismatch between health cert and commercial invoice, establishment audit non-conformance

examples.name: Australian Export Customs Clearance
examples.commonFailureReasons: Tariff classification dispute, incomplete export declaration, sanctions screening flag

examples.name: IPAFFS Pre-Notification (UK)
examples.avgDuration: Must be submitted 1-5 days before arrival
examples.commonFailureReasons: Late submission, health certificate number mismatch, commodity code error, missing CHED-P reference

examples.name: BCP Document & Identity Check
examples.commonFailureReasons: Health cert / CHED-P mismatch, seal number discrepancy, container markings inconsistent with documentation

examples.name: BCP Physical / SPS Inspection
examples.commonFailureReasons: Temperature excursion evidence, visual contamination, off-odour, positive lab result (E.coli O157, Salmonella), packaging damage

examples.name: UK Import Customs Clearance (CDS)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Incorrect tariff code, FTA preference claim rejected (origin proof insufficient), duty underpayment

examples.name: Cold Chain Temperature Verification
examples.authority: APHA / Port Health (mandatory for all chilled meat)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Temperature excursion >5C recorded, data logger malfunction, gaps in recording

examples.name: AU-UK FTA Preferential Origin Verification
examples.avgDuration: Auto-processed with customs declaration (or 5-10 days if audited)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Insufficient origin documentation, product not wholly obtained (mixed-origin feed lot), declaration of origin wording non-compliant

examples._section: === BOTTLENECKS (The Tight Spots) with Risk + Net Impact ===

examples.name: Reefer Container Shortage (Brisbane)
examples.description: Global reefer container imbalance. Empty reefers accumulate in import-heavy regions (Europe, US) while AU faces chronic shortages Oct-Mar. Wait times for 40ft HC reefers extend to 2-3 weeks at peak.
examples.rootCause: Structural global imbalance in reefer repositioning; shipping lines prioritise headhaul profitability over empty returns
examples.mitigationStrategy: Forward booking 6-8 weeks ahead; multi-carrier agreements; SOC (shipper-owned container) fleet for top 5 lanes
examples.workaroundDescription: Switch to Melbourne or Sydney port if Brisbane reefer availability <80%; accept 20ft reefer at higher per-unit cost

examples.name: Suez Canal Disruption / Red Sea Re-routing
examples.description: Houthi attacks force vessels via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 days and AUD 8,000-15,000 per container in fuel/war-risk surcharges. Even when open, canal congestion adds 1-2 days variability.
examples.rootCause: Regional military conflict; shipping lane chokepoint vulnerability
examples.mitigationStrategy: War risk insurance; dual-route pricing in contracts; shelf-life buffer planning for Cape route
examples.workaroundDescription: Cape of Good Hope routing. Requires shelf-life >55 days at dispatch.

examples.name: BCP Inspection Queue & Lab Turnaround
examples.description: UK BTOM physical inspection rates for AU beef run 15-30%. Lab sample turnaround 2-5 days. BCP has limited reefer holding. Christmas/bank holidays see skeleton staffing.
examples.rootCause: BTOM inspection regime; limited BCP capacity; lab resource constraints
examples.mitigationStrategy: Pre-arrival documentation quality checks; avoid Monday/Friday arrivals; maintain trusted trader status

examples.name: UK Cold Storage Capacity Shortage
examples.description: UK chilled storage operating at 92-96% capacity nationally. Christmas hits 99%+. When tight, rates spike 30-50% and consignments redirect to further facilities. Energy costs (+40% since 2022) stalling new builds.
examples.typicalDelay: 1-3 days (finding capacity) plus additional transport if redirected
examples.rootCause: Structural underinvestment in UK cold chain infrastructure; energy cost inflation; planning constraints
examples.mitigationStrategy: 12-month storage contract with guaranteed allocation; pre-book 8 weeks for peak; maintain secondary facility
examples.workaroundDescription: Redirect to secondary cold store (Grimsby/Avonmouth) at +15% transport cost; short-term reefer storage at port (max 5 days)

examples.name: IPAFFS Pre-Notification Errors
examples.description: IPAFFS system error-prone: commodity code lookup inconsistent, health cert cross-referencing fails silently, strict deadlines. 8% rejection rate on first submission.
examples.rootCause: IPAFFS system UX quality; inconsistent commodity code mapping
examples.mitigationStrategy: Pre-submission validation checklist; dedicated IPAFFS-trained staff; submit 5 days before arrival

examples.name: UK Retail Demand Spike (BBQ / Christmas)
examples.description: Demand spikes +25-35% BBQ season, +15-20% Christmas. Demand-led cost increases cascade: higher freight, storage premium, haulage surcharges. 15-30% total chain cost increase.
examples.typicalDelay: No delay — but cost increase 15-30% across chain
examples.rootCause: Predictable seasonal demand amplified by just-in-time retail stocking
examples.mitigationStrategy: Forward contracts 3-6 months ahead; pre-position buffer stock; negotiate annual freight rates

examples.name: Port of Felixstowe Congestion
examples.description: UKs largest container port (48% of trade). Periodic congestion from vessel bunching, weather, industrial action, IT outages. Reefer plug capacity: 2,400 points for ~4,000 weekly reefer moves.
examples.seasonality: Worse Oct-Dec (pre-Christmas surge) and during North Sea storms
examples.rootCause: Port capacity vs demand imbalance; limited berth windows; reefer plug infrastructure
examples.mitigationStrategy: Divert to London Gateway/Southampton if congestion >3 days; use inland rail clearance at DIRFT
examples.workaroundDescription: Diversion to London Gateway (30 miles south) — adds 1 day transport but avoids port queue

examples.name: Cold Chain Temperature Breach Risk
examples.description: Temperature excursions during container handling transitions. Reefer offline during crane lifts (3-5 min). Tropical ports (Brisbane 30-38C, Singapore 32-35C) cause rapid warming if pre-cool insufficient or seals degraded.
examples.typicalDelay: No delay — but potential total loss (AUD 185,000+ per container)
examples.seasonality: Worse Dec-Feb (peak ambient temperatures during loading/transhipment)
examples.rootCause: Container handling transitions; reefer power disconnection during crane ops; seal degradation
examples.mitigationStrategy: Pre-trip inspection; dual data loggers; pre-cool to -1C (thermal buffer); minimise Singapore dwell

examples.name: FTA Origin Proof Complexity
examples.description: AU-UK FTA preferential tariff (0% vs 12% MFN). Self-declaration regime means exporter bears verification burden. HMRC retrospective audits (up to 5 years) can reclaim duties + penalties.
examples.typicalDelay: No delay — but retrospective duty clawback risk GBP 25,000-100,000 per audit
examples.rootCause: Self-declaration origin regime complexity; wholly-obtained vs substantial transformation rules
examples.mitigationStrategy: Full farm-to-slaughter traceability; quarterly origin compliance audits; annual legal review

examples.cascadeEffect: Shelf-life erosion reduces UK retail window; potential price markdown or stock rejection
Shelf-life drops below 45 days; war risk surcharge passed to buyer; multiple containers affected simultaneously

examples.regulatoryConsequence: Increased future inspection frequency if non-conformance found
examples.cascadeEffect: Demurrage accumulates; cold store booking missed; retailer delivery delayed
Redirected to secondary facility; higher transport; container detained at port GBP 120/day

examples.regulatoryConsequence: APHA investigation; increased inspection rate 6 months; establishment review
examples.cascadeEffect: Total container loss; insurance claim 3-6 months; APHA increases ALL AU beef inspection rate 15% to 30%; reputational damage with retailer
Margin compressed from 12% to 2-4%; competitive pressure from South American suppliers if passed to retailer

examples.statement: Chilled beef shipped Brisbane to Felixstowe via Suez (Singapore transhipment) will arrive within 35 days of dispatch 80% of the time, with 95% arriving within 40 days
examples.measurementMethod: Container GPS tracking gate-out Brisbane to gate-in Felixstowe
examples.dataSource: Hapag-Lloyd container tracking API + SCT Logistics booking records 2023-2025
examples.learningNote: Red Sea instability adds 2-3 day variability even when Suez open. Hapag schedule reliability declined 72% to 65% in 2025.
examples.nextIteration: Refine to 36-day P80 target for 2026; add carrier reliability weighting

examples.statement: Chilled beef will arrive at UK cold store with minimum 50 days remaining shelf life, providing 45+ days for UK retail distribution
examples.baselineValue: 90-day total shelf life; 3 days processing + 35 days transit = 38 days consumed
examples.predictedValue: 52 days remaining at Daventry DC arrival
examples.measurementMethod: Shelf-life = slaughter date + 90 days - arrival date at Daventry
examples.dataSource: Darling Downs dispatch records + Lineage Logistics intake records
examples.actualOutcome: 2025 full year: mean 51 days remaining; 72% had >50 days; 3 shipments <45 days (all Suez-disrupted)
examples.learningNote: Cape reroutes consume shelf-life buffer entirely. Need 95-day product for Cape or switch to frozen.
examples.nextIteration: Model separately: Suez route (52-day) vs Cape route (40-day). If Cape probability >20%, frozen required.

examples.statement: UK cold storage costs will increase 8-12% annually through 2028 driven by energy costs and capacity constraints, reaching GBP 32-36 per pallet-week by end 2027
examples.baselineValue: GBP 26 per pallet-week (Jan 2026 Lineage contract rate)
examples.measurementMethod: Quarterly contract rate review + UK Cold Chain Federation annual survey
examples.dataSource: UK Cold Chain Federation 2025; Lineage contract history; BEIS energy price projections
examples.learningNote: Energy is ~60% of cold store cost. Government support schemes expiring 2026 will accelerate. New builds (Northampton, Dartford) may ease capacity by 2028.

examples.statement: APHA physical inspection rate for AU beef will decrease from 15% to 5% by 2028 as Australia achieves trusted country status under evolving UK SPS framework
examples.measurementMethod: Published APHA inspection rate data + DEFRA policy announcements
examples.dataSource: DEFRA BTOM review docs; APHA quarterly stats; AU-UK SPS Committee minutes
examples.learningNote: Highly dependent on UK government policy. If AU food safety incident occurs, rate could increase. Low confidence reflects political uncertainty.

examples.statement: Brisbane reefer availability will remain below 85% utilisation Oct-Mar, causing 60% of shipments to experience 3+ day equipment wait
examples.baselineValue: 2024-25 peak: 65% of shipments experienced 3+ day wait; utilisation hit 92%
examples.predictedValue: 60% affected (slight improvement from new Hapag fleet additions)
examples.measurementMethod: Days from booking confirmation to container available for pre-cool
examples.dataSource: SCT booking records; Hapag equipment dashboard; Port of Brisbane reefer yard data
examples.variance: +10% — underestimated Chinese reefer demand competing for same pool
examples.learningNote: Chinese pork/seafood exports draw reefers to Asia, reducing Brisbane availability. Hapag added 500 units but demand grew faster.
examples.nextIteration: Factor in Chinese export volume forecasts; model SOC fleet break-even for top 5 lanes

examples.name: Suez Canal Full Closure — Cape Reroute Required
examples.triggerCondition: Suez Canal closed to commercial shipping for >14 days
examples.assumptions: Cape routing available; no additional chokepoint conflict; carrier capacity maintained
examples.costChange: +AUD 12,000-18,000 per container (fuel + war risk + schedule disruption)
examples.shelfLifeImpact: Remaining drops to 38-42 days — below 45-day retailer minimum for some buyers
examples.mitigationPlan: Switch to frozen; negotiate retailer 40-day acceptance at 5% discount; activate Cape pricing
examples.predictiveSignals: Houthi attack frequency increasing; war risk surcharge announcements; vessel rerouting on MarineTraffic

examples.name: Christmas Peak Crunch — Storage + Demand Collision
examples.triggerCondition: Nov-Dec with UK cold storage >97% AND retail demand +20% AND Felixstowe >90% berth occupancy
examples.assumptions: Normal weather; no industrial action; UK economy stable
examples.costChange: +30-50% cold storage; +15% UK haulage; +10% demurrage risk
examples.mitigationPlan: Pre-position 4 weeks buffer by end-Oct; book cold store by Aug; secondary at Grimsby; arrivals Tue-Thu
examples.predictiveSignals: Cold store >93% by mid-Oct; Felixstowe berth >85%; carrier reliability <60%

examples.name: Temperature Breach at Singapore — BCP Rejection
examples.triggerCondition: Reefer compressor fails during Singapore transhipment; product >5C for >2 hours; detected at Felixstowe BCP
examples.assumptions: Data logger functional; APHA inspects temp records; breach genuine
examples.mitigationPlan: Insurance claim (80% recovery); carrier claim Hague-Visby; demand reefer pre-trip records; consider direct service
examples.predictiveSignals: Reefer unit age >8 years; Singapore ambient >34C; transhipment dwell >48 hours; marginal compressor PTI

examples.name: UK BTOM Tightening — Inspection Rates Doubled
examples.triggerCondition: UK announces Phase 3 BTOM: SPS physical checks on non-EU meat from 15% to 30%
examples.assumptions: UK prioritises food safety; no AU SPS equivalence agreement; farming lobby pressure
examples.costChange: +GBP 3,000-5,000 per consignment in BCP fees and demurrage
examples.mitigationPlan: Lobby via AMIC; accelerate trusted trader; invest in pre-shipment testing; consider alternative UK ports
examples.predictiveSignals: DEFRA consultation papers; parliamentary food safety reports; farming lobby activity

examples.name: Shelf-Life Erosion leads to Retailer Rejection / Markdown
examples.description: Any delay consuming shelf life pushes remaining days below retailer thresholds. Major UK supermarkets require 45+ days at intake. Below 40: rejected or 15-25% markdown. Below 30: unsaleable to retail, diverted to food service at 40% loss.
examples.triggerDelay: Immediate — every day of delay erodes shelf life by one day
examples.affectedParties: Importer (bears rejection cost); exporter (reputational, future order risk)
examples.preventionAction: Maintain minimum 55-day shelf life at dispatch; switch to frozen for >40 day transit routes

examples.name: Demand-Led Cost Escalation leads to Margin Compression
examples.description: Multiple cost increases compound: freight +15%, storage +35%, demurrage GBP 120/day, haulage +25%, insurance +10%. Total landed cost +20-40% during peaks. Margin compressed from target 12% to 2-4%. Cannot fully pass through (contractual price locks).
examples.affectedParties: Exporter (margin compression); importer (higher CIF duty base); cold store (revenue up but capacity stressed)
examples.preventionAction: Annual contract rates; retailer cost-escalation clauses; AUD/GBP forward hedging

examples.name: Cold Storage Overflow leads to Container Detention at Port
examples.description: When Daventry DC full, containers cannot be stripped. Sit at Felixstowe or on chassis. Detention GBP 85/day starts day 5. Multiple containers backing up takes 2-3 weeks to clear. Port reefer plug scarcity means containers may lose power.
examples.triggerDelay: 3-5 days after cold store reaches capacity
examples.affectedParties: Importer (detention); port (yard congestion); shipping line (equipment off-hire)
examples.preventionAction: Secondary cold store pre-arranged; stagger arrivals; pre-Christmas stock build from October

examples.name: Temperature Breach leads to Industry-Wide Inspection Increase
examples.description: ONE bad container triggers APHA to increase inspection rate for ALL Australian beef imports (not just offending exporter). Rate increases 15% to 30% for 6+ months. Most damaging unintended consequence: one exporter's failure affects entire AU industry to UK.
examples.affectedParties: ALL AU beef exporters to UK; UK importers (increased delays/costs); APHA (doubled workload)
examples.preventionAction: Zero-tolerance pre-trip inspection; dual data loggers; avoid transhipment in peak summer; industry reefer quality programme

examples.name: Claims History leads to Insurance Premium Increase
examples.description: Marine cargo premiums increase 15-25% following total-loss claim. War risk adds 0.05-0.15% of cargo value. After 2+ claims in 12 months, insurers may impose deductible increases or decline renewal. AUD 8,000-15,000 additional annually.
examples.affectedParties: Exporter (premium payer); importer (CIF increase); insurer (claims ratio)
examples.preventionAction: Risk management programme; loss prevention investment; multi-insurer panel

examples.name: Repeated Delays leads to Retailer Switches to Competitor
examples.description: UK supermarket buyers operate on strict KPIs: OTIF >95%, shelf-life compliance >98%. If AU beef consistently fails (3+ in 6 months), buyer diversifies to South American or Irish beef. Once lost, re-listing takes 12-18 months through re-tender.
examples.affectedParties: Exporter (lost revenue, stranded capacity); importer (must find alternative or lose retailer)
examples.preventionAction: Dedicated retailer account manager; proactive delay notification; UK buffer stock; dual-corridor (Brisbane + Melbourne)

examples.name: Annual Reefer Crunch Cycle (Brisbane Oct-Mar)
examples.description: Every year Brisbane reefer availability drops sharply Oct-Mar as AU meat/dairy/fruit export season peaks. Troughs Dec-Jan. Recovers by April.
examples.leadIndicators: Hapag equipment dashboard >80% utilisation by September; Port reefer yard occupancy reports; carrier surcharge announcements
examples.typicalImpact: 3-14 day equipment wait; 15-25% of shipments require alternative port/carrier; AUD 2,000-5,000 premium per container
examples.recommendedAction: Book reefer equipment 8 weeks ahead for Oct-Mar; multi-carrier agreements; evaluate SOC fleet

examples.name: North Sea Storm Delays (Felixstowe Oct-Feb)
examples.description: North Sea storms cause 1-4 day vessel delays, 3-5 events per winter. Port closure (wind >40 knots) causes vessel bunching and 3-7 day post-storm congestion.
examples.leadIndicators: Met Office severe weather warnings; Felixstowe operational status; vessel schedule delays >24 hours
examples.typicalImpact: 1-4 day vessel delay; 3-7 day post-storm congestion; demurrage; missed BCP slots
examples.recommendedAction: Monitor Met Office 5-day; pre-book BCP with 2-day buffer; London Gateway as storm-alternative

examples.name: UK BBQ Season Demand Surge (May-Aug)
examples.description: UK beef demand +25-35% during BBQ season. Steak cuts highest uplift. Retailers order 8-12 weeks ahead. Late surge (unexpected heatwave) causes 40-60% spot premium.
examples.leadIndicators: Met Office seasonal outlook; retailer forward order volumes Mar-Apr; supermarket promotional calendars Jan
examples.typicalImpact: Volume +25-35%; price premium 10-20% contract, 40-60% spot; cold storage >95%; haulage surcharges
examples.recommendedAction: Secure forward contracts by Feb; pre-build 2-week buffer by end-Apr; lock storage and transport by Mar

examples.name: AUD/GBP Exchange Rate Commodity Cycle
examples.description: AUD/GBP correlates with commodity prices (iron ore, coal, LNG). AUD strong = AU beef more expensive for UK = demand softens. AUD weak = competitive = demand rises. 18-24 month cycle.
examples.leadIndicators: Iron ore spot price trend; RBA policy statements; China PMI/steel demand; commodity futures curve
examples.typicalImpact: 5-12% landed cost variation; demand elasticity ~0.6; margin impact AUD 15,000-40,000 per container
examples.recommendedAction: Forward hedge 50% at budget rate; maintain GBP revenue account; quarterly retailer price adjustment

examples.description: Container HLCU-7294618 reefer compressor failed during Singapore transhipment. Temperature rose 0.5C to 7.2C over 4 hours. Data logger recorded +5C excursion for 2h 40m. Detected at Felixstowe BCP.
examples.rootCause: Reefer compressor age 9 years; Singapore yard unplugged 6 hours during peak congestion; genset not attached for cranage
examples.resolution: Container rejected by APHA. Product destroyed. Insurance AUD 148,000 (80%). Carrier claim AUD 12,000. Hapag committed max 3-hour unplug and mandatory genset for AU reefers.
examples.timeToDetect: 28 days (not detected until BCP data logger download)
examples.lessonLearned: Invest in real-time satellite reefer monitoring. Demand genset backup in carrier contracts. Consider direct service (no transhipment) for high-value.

examples.description: IPAFFS for HLCU-8831227 rejected: commodity code 0201.30.00 (chilled) but health cert specified 0202.30.90 (frozen). Exporter switched chilled mid-booking without updating health cert.
examples.rootCause: Manual data entry; health cert for original frozen not updated; no pre-submission cross-check
examples.resolution: DAFF amended health cert (24-hour emergency). IPAFFS resubmitted. Container held 2 days. Demurrage GBP 240.
examples.lessonLearned: Mandatory pre-submission cross-check: health cert vs IPAFFS vs commercial invoice. Add to SOP. Consider IPAFFS API auto-populate.

examples.description: Storm Eowyn closed Felixstowe 36 hours. 12 vessels queued. Post-storm 4-day congestion. Reefer plug availability dropped to 60%. Three AU beef containers waited 5 days. Christmas peak compounded everything.
examples.rootCause: North Sea storm (force majeure); vessel bunching; limited reefer capacity; holiday staffing
examples.resolution: One container diverted to London Gateway (1 day late). Two waited 5 days at Felixstowe. One retailer applied 10% markdown (<45 days shelf life). Total: GBP 14,500.
examples.lessonLearned: Pre-Christmas arrivals target Dec 10th (10-day buffer). London Gateway pre-approved as alternative. Real-time port congestion dashboard needed.

examples.triggerCondition: Temperature breach detected at BCP — product >5C for >1 hour
examples.prescribedActions: Quarantine container, notify insurer, initiate carrier claim, alert APHA, photograph product
examples.decisionAuthority: Accept/reject; authorise destruction; file claim up to AUD 200,000

examples.triggerCondition: Total loss confirmed; insurance >AUD 100,000; carrier liability dispute
examples.prescribedActions: Formal insurance claim, carrier liability letter, AMIC notification, root cause investigation, corrective action to APHA, review Hapag contract
examples.decisionAuthority: Authorise legal action; negotiate settlement; approve supply chain design changes; commit capital for monitoring

examples.prescribedActions: Identify rejection reason, contact DAFF if amended docs needed, correct and resubmit, notify BCP
examples.decisionAuthority: Resubmit IPAFFS; request amended health cert; authorise demurrage up to GBP 500

examples.triggerCondition: Port congestion >3 days AND shelf life at risk on 2+ containers
examples.prescribedActions: Assess port diversion, contact London Gateway/Southampton, negotiate emergency cold store, notify retailer, calculate shelf-life per container
examples.decisionAuthority: Authorise diversion (GBP 2,000-5,000); emergency haulage; negotiate retailer markdown vs rejection

examples.commodity: Chilled grass-fed beef — sirloin, ribeye, fillet (vacuum-packed primals)
